design_philosophy.core_loop: Gather resources, build military units, scout opponent positions, execute coordinated attacks while defending base
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Imperfect information and real-time decision-making under pressure; managing multiple units simultaneously while responding to threats
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command a faction's military forces in tactical real-time combat; experience asymmetric gameplay through three fundamentally different factions
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Extreme faction asymmetry by design: Terrans are adaptable and defensive; Protoss are powerful but expensive with limited production; Zerg are fast, fragile swarm-based. Demands mastery of matchup-specific strategies rather than universal approach. High-skill ceiling through multitasking and micro-management

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Campaign difficulty varies by mission design; multiplayer difficulty determined entirely by opponent skill. No explicit difficulty settings documented

progression.upgrade_system: Unit upgrades and tech tree advancement through buildings and research structures; faction-specific upgrade paths
progression.unlock_mechanism: Campaign missions unlock sequentially; multiplayer provides immediate access to all units for chosen faction
replayability_hooks: Three faction campaigns with distinct storylines and unit rosters
Asymmetric multiplayer requiring different strategic approaches per faction
Competitive ladder play with skill-based ranking
Skirmish mode against AI opponents with varying difficulties

world_building_techniques: High-quality pre-rendered cinematics integral to storyline
Campaign missions structured around narrative events
Three separate faction campaigns revealing story from different perspectives (Terran, Protoss, Zerg)
Character-driven military command structure (commanders leading campaign arcs)

boss_design.philosophy: Campaign missions feature enemy commander encounters and final base assaults rather than traditional boss fights; adapted to RTS genre with heavily fortified positions and specialized unit compositions
notable_bosses: Tassadar and Zeratul (Protoss leaders in their campaigns)

faction_indicators: Terran Dominion: militaristic human empire, expansionist colonial power
Protoss: ancient alien civilization, mysterious and isolationist, philosophical divide between Templar and Dark Templar
Zerg: hive-minded swarm collective, assimilationist, biological determinism
danger_signals: Zerg infestation as existential biological threat requiring containment
Protoss intervention viewed as alien invasion by Terrans despite protective intent
Civil conflict within Terran factions (Confederacy vs. Dominion vs. Raynor's rebels)
Kerrigan's transformation symbolizes loss of individual identity to collective
environmental_storytelling: Destroyed colonies showing Zerg devastation and Protoss culling
Tarsonis capital fall depicts cascade of faction collapse under dual threats
Unit design reflects faction philosophy (organic Zerg vs. mechanical Terran vs. psionic Protoss)
Map layouts show territorial claims and fortified positions of competing factions
